title: Overview of MySQL Data Types
summary: Complete set of MySQL data types: Integer, Decimal, Date/Time, String (CHAR vs VARCHAR), LOBs, Binary, plus NULL and DEFAULT, ENUM constraints.
content_html:
Overview of MySQL Data Types

When creating a table in MySQL, each column must be assigned one data type (data type) determines what types of values are allowed to be stored. Choosing the right data type helps optimize memory and ensure data integrity.

1. Integer Types

TypeBytesRange (Signed)Notes
TINYINT1-128 đến 127Used for boolean flags
SMALLINT2-32,768 đến 32,767Small quantity
MEDIUMINT3-8,388,608 đến 8,388,607
INT4-2,147,483,648 đến 2,147,483,647Most popular
BIGINT8±9.2 × 10¹⁸Very large number

Add UNSIGNED to start from 0 (non-negative), e.g.: INT UNSIGNED for range 0 to 4,294,967,295.

2. Real number types (Decimal/Float Types)

TypeDescriptionFor example
DECIMAL(p,s)Exact decimal number. p = total digits, s = digits after decimal pointDECIMAL(8,2) → 123456.78
FLOATFloating point numbers (4 bytes), not absolutely accurateScience, measurement
DOUBLEDouble precision (8 bytes)Scientific calculation

Important note: Use DECIMAL for currency and financial data because it does not suffer from rounding errors like FLOAT.

3. Date/Time Types

TypeFormatScope
DATEYYYY-MM-DD1000-01-01 đến 9999-12-31
TIMEHH:MM:SS-838:59:59 đến 838:59:59
DATETIMEYYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS1000-01-01 đến 9999-12-31
TIMESTAMPYYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS1970-01-01 to 2038 (Unix epoch)
YEARYYYY1901 đến 2155

4. String Types: CHAR vs VARCHAR

This is the most important difference in string type:

CHAR(n)

Fixed-length: Always use exactly n characters, regardless of content.

For example: CHAR(15) save 'Hello':

H|e|l|l|o|_|_|_|_|_|_|_|_|_|_

(5 characters + 10 padding spaces)

✅ Faster when the column has a fixed length (postal code, country code)

VARCHAR(n)

Variable-length: Only use the exact number of bytes needed + 1-2 bytes to save the length.

For example: VARCHAR(15) save 'Hello':

H|e|l|l|o

(5 characters, no padding)

✅ Save memory when length varies (person's name, address)

When to use CHAR? When data always has a fixed length: postal code (4 characters), country code (2 characters), fixed format phone number.

When to use VARCHAR? When length varies: person's name, email address, product description.

5. LOB (Large Object) type

TypeLimitUsed for
TEXT65,535 bytes (~64KB)Short-medium text
MEDIUMTEXT16,777,215 bytes (~16MB)Articles, documents
LONGTEXT4,294,967,295 bytes (~4GB)Books, great content
BLOB65,535 bytesBinary data (small image)
MEDIUMBLOB~16MBPhotos, short audio
LONGBLOB~4GBVideos, large files

6. Binary Types

BINARY(n) and VARBINARY(n) Similar to CHAR and VARCHAR but stores raw bytes (not text). Used for encrypted data, hashes, binary UUIDs.

7. NULL — "unknown" value

NULL is not:
  • Number 0
  • Empty string ''
  • Whitespace ' '
NULL is "unknown value" — an unknown value.

MySQL uses ternary logic (three-valued logic) with NULL:

ExpressionResultsExplanation
NULL = NULLNULL (not TRUE)Don't know = don't know → can't compare
NULL = 0NULLI don't know if it's equal to 0 → I don't know
NULL + 5NULLCalculate with unknown → unknown

Correct way to check for NULL: Use IS NULL or IS NOT NULL, never use = NULL.

SELECT * FROM Student WHERE PhoneNumber IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM Student WHERE PhoneNumber IS NOT NULL;

Column declaration NULL is not allowed:

name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL

8. DEFAULT constraint

DEFAULT sets the default value when INSERT does not provide a value for that column:

CREATE TABLE Staff (
    StaffID INT NOT NULL,
    StaffName VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    Gender ENUM('M','F','Other') DEFAULT 'Other',
    IsActive TINYINT DEFAULT 1,
    JoinDate DATE DEFAULT (CURRENT_DATE)
);

When INSERT does not provide Gender → automatically save 'Other'. When INSERT does not provide IsActive → autosave 1.

9. ENUM type

ENUM limits a column to only one of the available listed values:

Gender ENUM('M', 'F', 'Other') DEFAULT 'Other'

Advantage: Ensures valid data (cannot enter 'Male', 'Female' or value not in the list). MySQL stores it internally in integers (1, 2, 3...) so it is very compact.

Practical example from the lecture — Staff table with full constraints:

CREATE TABLE Staff (
    StaffID     INT           NOT NULL,
    StaffName   VARCHAR(100)  NOT NULL,
    Gender      ENUM('M','F','Other') DEFAULT 'Other',
    BranchID    INT           NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (StaffID),
    FOREIGN KEY (BranchID) REFERENCES Branch(BranchID)
);

Example PropertyInspection table from the 3NF case study:

CREATE TABLE PropertyInspection (
    InspectionID  INT           NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
    PropertyID    INT           NOT NULL,
    StaffID       INT           NOT NULL,
    InspDate      DATE          NOT NULL,
    InspTime      TIME,
    Comments      TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (InspectionID),
    FOREIGN KEY (PropertyID) REFERENCES Property(PropertyID),
    FOREIGN KEY (StaffID) REFERENCES Staff(StaffID)
);
